book_summary: Hebrews 1-13 has full chapter coverage with source-audited-draft restored translation and mystical companion layers. The book preserves Son as Divine speech, flesh-and-blood solidarity, merciful high priesthood, Sabbath-rest, Melchizedek priesthood, new covenant inwardness, tabernacle/conscience cleansing, once-for-all offering, severe warnings with pastoral safeguards, ancestral trust, endurance, unshakable reign, hospitality, prisoner solidarity, and embodied grace ethics.
translation_notes: Hebrews must not be read as Christianity replacing Judaism. Its argument works through Israel's Scriptures, priesthood, covenant, tabernacle, and promise language.
New covenant quotation in Hebrews 8 is explicitly to house of Israel and house of Judah; preserve continuity.
Copy/shadow language describes cultic/heavenly pattern relation, not contempt for Torah, temple, or Jewish worship.
Melchizedek should be handled as a Genesis/Psalm argument, not speculative mythology.
Blood/life language should avoid gore-fixation and highlight life-offering, covenant, conscience cleansing, and access.
Warning passages must be paired with pastoral safeguards: encouragement, confidence, better things, access, grace, endurance, and preserving life.
Hebrews 11 honors Israel's ancestors and women of trust; do not erase Jewish identity.
Discipline language in Hebrews 12 must not justify abuse; it points toward healing and peaceful fruit of justice.
Leadership language in Hebrews 13 is accountable and non-domineering; sibling-love, hospitality, prisoner solidarity, doing good, and sharing frame the ethic.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Hebrews authorship/source-setting remains unknown and should be noted in second-pass metadata.
Hebrews' extensive Septuagint citation network requires citation-level source witness audit.
Hebrews 10:5 Psalm 40 body prepared vs Hebrew ears opened requires source-witness audit.
Hebrews warning passages 6 and 10 require pastoral reception-history safeguards.
Hebrews 8-10 covenant/priesthood language requires anti-supersessionist review.
Hebrews 11 ancestral examples require Jewish-context preservation audit.
next_actions: Only Revelation remains in the New Testament. Treat Revelation as a single deep symbolic/apocalyptic pass with anti-violence, anti-empire, anti-paranoia, and genre safeguards.
